records.ety: [OE.cortin, curtin,fr. OF. cortine, curtine, F. courtine, LL. cortina, curtian (in senses 1 and 2), also, small court, small inclosure surrounded by walls, from cortis court. See Court.]

defs: A hanging screen intended to darken or conceal, and admitting of being drawn back or up, and reclosed at pleasure; esp., drapery of cloth or lace hanging round a bed or at a window; in theaters, and like places, a movable screen for concealing the stage.
That part of the rampart and parapet which is between two bastions or two gates. See Illustrations of Ravelin and Bastion.
That part of a wall of a building which is between two pavilions, towers, etc.
A flag; an ensign; -- in contempt.
quotes: A curtain lecture is worth all the sermons in the world for teaching the virtues of patience and long-suffering.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Curtained (kr"tnd; 48); p. pr. & vb. n. Curtaining.]

defs: To inclose as with curtains; to furnish with curtains.
quotes: So when the sun in bed Curtained with cloudy red.
